blockingIssues.description: The fix handoff maps VAL-CROSS-002 through VAL-CROSS-006 entirely to already-committed artifacts from `mission-control-ui`, `mission-sync-recovery`, and `mission-foundation`, then explicitly states no new tests were added because the proof was already present on the checked-in head. Commit `bce5ae0` records scrutiny and library artifacts only, so older milestone evidence remains the sole basis for the claimed cross-flow coverage instead of feature-owned checked-in proof.

The cited Mission-GUI-TEST runtime evidence still says the unsafe pause, kill-worker, and related variants were validated with isolated in-memory renderer-state injection plus focused tests to avoid mutating the shared Mission run. That leaves the same-session Mission-GUI-TEST end-to-end proof incomplete for pause -> continue, daemon/factoryd failure -> continue, and kill-worker -> paused or resumable Mission flows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify `mission-verification-worker` to state whether proof-only re-reviews are allowed and how workers must document reuse of pre-existing evidence instead of producing new automated tests or Mission-GUI-TEST artifacts.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `mission-cross-flow-lifecycle-proof` handoff marked the procedure as followed while adding no tests and relying entirely on earlier milestone artifacts, even though the skill currently reads like a test-authoring workflow that requires fresh proof or explicit gap reporting.
